question_text: In a parallel circuit with 3 identical bulbs, what happens if you remove one bulb from its socket?

options.label: A
options.text: All the remaining bulbs go out

options.label: B
options.text: The remaining bulbs stay at the same brightness

explanation: In a parallel circuit, each bulb has its own separate path for current. If you remove one bulb, the others still have their own complete circuits, so they remain at the same brightness.
